children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be in the Department a bureau to be known as the “Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services”.

children.label: A
children.text: shall report directly to the Deputy Secretary;

children.label: B
children.text: shall have a minimum of 5 years of management experience; and

children.label: C
children.text: shall be paid at the same level as the Assistant Secretary of the Bureau of Border Security.

children.label: A
children.text: shall establish the policies for performing such functions as are transferred to the Director by this section or this chapter or otherwise vested in the Director by law;

children.label: B
children.text: shall oversee the administration of such policies;

children.label: C
children.text: shall advise the Deputy Secretary with respect to any policy or operation of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services that may affect the Bureau of Border Security of the Department, including potentially conflicting policies or operations;

children.label: D
children.text: shall establish national immigration services policies and priorities;

children.label: E
children.text: shall meet regularly with the Ombudsman described in to correct serious service problems identified by the Ombudsman; andsection 272 of this title

children.label: F
children.text: shall establish procedures requiring a formal response to any recommendations submitted in the Ombudsman’s annual report to Congress within 3 months after its submission to Congress.

children.label: i
children.text: gain some experience in all the major functions performed by such bureau; and

children.label: ii
children.text: work in at least one field office and one service center of such bureau.

children.heading: Report
children.label: B Report
children.text: Not later than 2 years after the effective date specified in section 455, the Secretary shall submit a report to Congress on the implementation of such program.1

children.heading: Pilot initiatives for backlog elimination
children.label: 5 Pilot initiatives for backlog elimination
children.text: The Director of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services is authorized to implement innovative pilot initiatives to eliminate any remaining backlog in the processing of immigration benefit applications, and to prevent any backlog in the processing of such applications from recurring, in accordance with . Such initiatives may include measures such as increasing personnel, transferring personnel to focus on areas with the largest potential for backlog, and streamlining paperwork.section 1573(a) of title 8

children.heading: Transfer of functions from Commissioner
children.label: b Transfer of functions from Commissioner

children.text: Adjudications of immigrant visa petitions.
Adjudications of naturalization petitions.
Adjudications of asylum and refugee applications.
Adjudications performed at service centers.
All other adjudications performed by the Immigration and Naturalization Service immediately before the effective date specified in section 455.1

children.heading: Chief of Policy and Strategy
children.label: c Chief of Policy and Strategy

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be a position of Chief of Policy and Strategy for the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.label: A
children.text: making policy recommendations and performing policy research and analysis on immigration services issues; and

children.label: B
children.text: coordinating immigration policy issues with the Chief of Policy and Strategy for the Bureau of Border Security of the Department.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be a principal legal advisor to the Director of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.label: A
children.text: providing specialized legal advice, opinions, determinations, regulations, and any other assistance to the Director of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services with respect to legal matters affecting the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services; and

children.label: B
children.text: representing the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services in visa petition appeal proceedings before the Executive Office for Immigration Review.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be a Budget Officer for the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.heading: In general2So in original. There is no subpar. (B).2
children.label: A In general2So in original. There is no subpar. (B).2
i
children.text: formulating and executing the budget of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services;

children.label: ii
children.text: financial management of the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services; and

children.label: iii
children.text: collecting all payments, fines, and other debts for the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.heading: Chief of Office of Citizenship
children.label: f Chief of Office of Citizenship

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: There shall be a position of Chief of the Office of Citizenship for the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: 2 Functions
children.text: The Chief of the Office of Citizenship for the Bureau of Citizenship and Immigration Services shall be responsible for promoting instruction and training on citizenship responsibilities for aliens interested in becoming naturalized citizens of the United States, including the development of educational materials.
